metadata.title: Decode the Hidden Curriculum Every College Syllabus Hides
metadata.note: CLI video in the REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(slate) · B05 CHANGE · B06 slate · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: Every syllabus is two documents. One is printed. The other is not. The printed one lists assignments and due dates. The hidden one tells you how to actually succeed — which signals matter, how faculty really grade, and what first-semester students almost always get wrong. This video shows you how to surface that second document with one CLI command.
beats.visual_intent: title card: two-column layout — 'What the syllabus SAYS' vs 'What it MEANS'; no terminal yet

beats.narration_text: One command. Paste the syllabus passage in, and ask Claude to find every hidden curriculum signal and build a five-row translation table — term, what it suggests, what it means in college, the common first-semester mistake, and what to do instead.

props.command: claude "I'm going to paste a college syllabus passage. Identify every hidden curriculum signal — anything communicated implicitly, not explicitly — and generate a 5-row translation table: Term | What it suggests | What it means in college | Common first-semester mistake | What to do instead. Rows: Syllabus, Office Hours, Plagiarism, Final Exam, Faculty."

beats.narration_text: The output is a markdown file — hidden underscore curriculum dot md. Each row is a translation: what the term signals on the surface, what it actually means in college culture, the mistake most first-semester students make, and the specific action that replaces that mistake.

props.code:
# hidden_curriculum.md — translation table output

| Term        | What it suggests      | What it means in college         | Common mistake              | What to do instead          |
|-------------|-----------------------|----------------------------------|-----------------------------|-----------------------------|  
| Syllabus    | Rules document        | Faculty's contract with you      | Read once, ignore later     | Re-read before each unit    |
| Office Hours| Optional help session | Faculty-designated 1-on-1 time   | Never attend (feels needy)  | Go twice before first exam  |
| Plagiarism  | Copying from web      | Any unattributed intellectual use| Forget citation on draft    | Cite everything, ask first  |
| Final Exam  | Test at end of course | Cumulative mastery signal        | Cram the night before       | Start review in week 10     |
| Faculty     | Teacher or professor  | Expert + evaluator + gatekeeper  | Avoid as authority figure   | Build a professional relation|

beats.narration_text: The table lands. Five rows, five translations. Each one shows the gap between what a term signals and what it actually means when you're sitting in that classroom and your grade depends on it.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of the markdown table rendered in terminal — five rows fully visible, each column aligned

beats.narration_text: Good start. Now sharpen it. Ask Claude to add a sixth row — Participation — and for each row add a severity rating: how badly does getting this wrong typically damage your GPA in semester one.

props.command: claude "Add a 6th row for 'Participation' to the table. Also add a Severity column (Low/Medium/High) rating how badly getting each signal wrong damages GPA in semester 1."

beats.narration_text: The updated table adds Participation and the severity column. Office Hours and Plagiarism are high-severity signals that most first-semester students misread. The severity column is the part you actually want to print and stick to your desk.
beats.visual_intent: screen recording of the revised 6-row table with severity column — Office Hours and Plagiarism marked HIGH

beats.narration_text: The hidden curriculum is not mysterious. It is a set of implicit expectations that experienced students absorb over years. A translation table surfaces those expectations in one pass — so you can act on them in week one instead of learning them the hard way in week fourteen.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Hidden curriculum = implicit expectations'; table preview showing Severity column; 'Surface it in week 1'

beats.narration_text: Your move: paste your own syllabus into Claude and run this prompt. Add your specific course, your faculty name, and any language that felt confusing. Then book office hours for the two highest-severity items — before the first exam.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) paste your own syllabus, 2) filter by High severity, 3) book office hours in week 2

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
